title: Banana (medium, 118g)
content: Calories: 105 kcal. Protein: 1.3g. Carbs: 27g. Fat: 0.4g. Fiber: 3.1g. Rich in potassium (422mg), vitamin B6, and vitamin C. Glycemic index: 51 (low-medium).

title: Chicken Breast (grilled, 100g)
content: Calories: 165 kcal. Protein: 31g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 3.6g. One of the best lean protein sources. Contains niacin, vitamin B6, and selenium.

title: Brown Rice (cooked, 1 cup / 195g)
content: Calories: 216 kcal. Protein: 5g. Carbs: 45g. Fat: 1.8g. Fiber: 3.5g. Complex carbohydrate with low glycemic index (50). Good source of manganese and magnesium.

title: White Rice (cooked, 1 cup / 186g)
content: Calories: 206 kcal. Protein: 4.3g. Carbs: 45g. Fat: 0.4g. Fiber: 0.6g. High glycemic index (73). Good post-workout carb source for rapid glycogen replenishment.

title: Egg (whole, large, 50g)
content: Calories: 72 kcal. Protein: 6.3g. Carbs: 0.4g. Fat: 4.8g. Contains all essential amino acids. Rich in choline (147mg), vitamin D, and B12. Cholesterol: 186mg.

title: Salmon (Atlantic, cooked, 100g)
content: Calories: 208 kcal. Protein: 20g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 13g. High in omega-3 fatty acids (EPA 0.86g, DHA 1.1g). Excellent source of vitamin D and B12.

title: Broccoli (cooked, 1 cup / 156g)
content: Calories: 55 kcal. Protein: 3.7g. Carbs: 11g. Fat: 0.6g. Fiber: 5.1g. High in vitamin C (101mg), vitamin K, and folate. Contains sulforaphane with anti-inflammatory properties.

title: Sweet Potato (baked, medium / 114g)
content: Calories: 103 kcal. Protein: 2.3g. Carbs: 24g. Fat: 0.1g. Fiber: 3.8g. High in vitamin A (438% DV), vitamin C, and manganese. Medium glycemic index (63).

title: Greek Yogurt (plain, nonfat, 1 cup / 245g)
content: Calories: 100 kcal. Protein: 17g. Carbs: 6g. Fat: 0.7g. Excellent protein source. Contains probiotics for gut health. Rich in calcium (187mg) and B12.

title: Oatmeal (cooked, 1 cup / 234g)
content: Calories: 154 kcal. Protein: 5.4g. Carbs: 27g. Fat: 2.6g. Fiber: 4g. Contains beta-glucan soluble fiber which helps lower cholesterol. Low glycemic index (55).

title: Almonds (raw, 28g / 1 oz)
content: Calories: 161 kcal. Protein: 6g. Carbs: 6g. Fat: 14g. Fiber: 3.5g. High in vitamin E (7.3mg), magnesium (76mg), and healthy monounsaturated fats.

title: Avocado (medium, 150g)
content: Calories: 240 kcal. Protein: 3g. Carbs: 13g. Fat: 22g. Fiber: 10g. Rich in monounsaturated fats, potassium (728mg), and folate. Supports heart health.

title: Whole Wheat Bread (1 slice / 28g)
content: Calories: 69 kcal. Protein: 3.6g. Carbs: 12g. Fat: 1.1g. Fiber: 1.9g. Better glycemic response than white bread. Contains B vitamins and iron.

title: Apple (medium, 182g)
content: Calories: 95 kcal. Protein: 0.5g. Carbs: 25g. Fat: 0.3g. Fiber: 4.4g. Contains quercetin antioxidant. Low glycemic index (36). Good source of vitamin C.

title: Lentils (cooked, 1 cup / 198g)
content: Calories: 230 kcal. Protein: 18g. Carbs: 40g. Fat: 0.8g. Fiber: 15.6g. Excellent plant protein source. Rich in iron (6.6mg), folate, and potassium.

title: Whey Protein Powder (1 scoop / 30g)
content: Calories: 110-130 kcal. Protein: 24-27g. Carbs: 1-3g. Fat: 1-2g. Fast-digesting protein, ideal post-workout. Complete amino acid profile with high leucine content for muscle protein synthesis.

title: Pizza (cheese, 1 slice / 107g)
content: Calories: 285 kcal. Protein: 12g. Carbs: 36g. Fat: 10g. A typical whole medium pizza (8 slices) contains approximately 2,280 kcal total. High in sodium (~640mg per slice).

title: Hamburger (regular, with bun)
content: Calories: 354 kcal. Protein: 20g. Carbs: 29g. Fat: 17g. A fast-food double cheeseburger can contain 450-740 kcal. High in saturated fat and sodium.

title: French Fries (medium serving / 117g)
content: Calories: 365 kcal. Protein: 4g. Carbs: 44g. Fat: 17g. High glycemic index (75). Deep-fried preparation adds significant calories. A large serving can contain 500+ kcal.

title: Coca-Cola (12 oz / 355ml can)
content: Calories: 140 kcal. Protein: 0g. Carbs: 39g (all sugar). Fat: 0g. Contains 39g of added sugar. No nutritional value. Liquid calories do not promote satiety.

title: Quinoa (cooked, 1 cup / 185g)
content: Calories: 222 kcal. Protein: 8g. Carbs: 39g. Fat: 3.6g. Fiber: 5.2g. Complete plant protein (contains all 9 essential amino acids). Rich in iron, magnesium, and manganese.

title: Spinach (raw, 1 cup / 30g)
content: Calories: 7 kcal. Protein: 0.9g. Carbs: 1.1g. Fat: 0.1g. Extremely nutrient-dense: high in vitamin K (145mcg), vitamin A, folate, iron, and calcium. Anti-inflammatory properties.

title: Tofu (firm, 100g)
content: Calories: 76 kcal. Protein: 8g. Carbs: 1.9g. Fat: 4.8g. Good plant protein source with all essential amino acids. Rich in calcium and iron. Low in saturated fat.

title: Peanut Butter (2 tbsp / 32g)
content: Calories: 188 kcal. Protein: 8g. Carbs: 6g. Fat: 16g. Fiber: 2g. High in monounsaturated fats, vitamin E, and magnesium. Calorie-dense — easy to overconsume.

title: Milk (whole, 1 cup / 244ml)
content: Calories: 149 kcal. Protein: 8g. Carbs: 12g. Fat: 8g. Contains calcium (276mg), vitamin D, B12, and riboflavin. Lactose content may cause digestive issues for some.

title: Tuna (canned in water, 100g)
content: Calories: 116 kcal. Protein: 26g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 0.8g. Very lean protein source. High in selenium, niacin, and B12. Contains omega-3 fatty acids. Limit to 2-3 servings/week due to mercury.

title: Olive Oil (1 tbsp / 14g)
content: Calories: 119 kcal. Protein: 0g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 14g. Rich in monounsaturated fats (oleic acid) and antioxidant polyphenols. Supports heart health. Use for cooking under 375°F.

title: Cottage Cheese (low-fat, 1 cup / 226g)
content: Calories: 163 kcal. Protein: 28g. Carbs: 6g. Fat: 2.3g. Excellent casein protein source (slow-digesting). Ideal before bed for sustained amino acid release. Rich in selenium and B12.

title: Dark Chocolate (70-85% cocoa, 1 oz / 28g)
content: Calories: 170 kcal. Protein: 2.2g. Carbs: 13g. Fat: 12g. Fiber: 3.1g. Contains flavonoids with cardiovascular benefits. High in magnesium and iron. Moderate consumption recommended.

title: Chicken Thigh (boneless, skinless, cooked, 100g)
content: Calories: 209 kcal. Protein: 26g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 10.9g. Higher in fat and more flavorful than chicken breast. Good source of zinc and iron. Good budget-friendly protein option.

title: Rice and Curry (typical Sri Lankan meal, 1 plate)
content: Calories: 550-750 kcal per plate. Protein: 15-25g. Carbs: 70-90g. Fat: 20-35g. Nutritional value varies significantly based on curry type, coconut milk usage, and rice portion. A typical plate includes 1-1.5 cups rice with 2-3 curries.

title: Chicken Fried Rice (1 plate / 350g)
content: Calories: 450-550 kcal. Protein: 18-22g. Carbs: 55-65g. Fat: 15-22g. Calories depend on oil quantity and portion size. Restaurant versions tend to be higher in fat and sodium.

title: Roti/Chapati (1 piece / 35g)
content: Calories: 104 kcal. Protein: 3.5g. Carbs: 18g. Fat: 2.5g. Fiber: 2.7g. Made from whole wheat flour. Lower glycemic index than white rice. Good source of iron and B vitamins.

title: Dal/Dhal (cooked, 1 cup / 240ml)
content: Calories: 180-220 kcal. Protein: 12-16g. Carbs: 28-35g. Fat: 3-8g. Fiber: 8-12g. Excellent plant protein source. Rich in iron, folate, and potassium. Calories increase with coconut milk or ghee.

title: Coconut Milk (1 cup / 240ml)
content: Calories: 445 kcal. Protein: 4.6g. Carbs: 6.4g. Fat: 48g (saturated: 43g). Very calorie-dense. Light coconut milk has approximately 140 kcal/cup. Used in many South Asian curries.

title: String Hoppers / Idiyappam (1 piece / 30g)
content: Calories: 100 kcal. Protein: 2g. Carbs: 22g. Fat: 0.3g. Made from rice flour. Light and easily digestible. Typically served with curry. 8-10 string hoppers is a common serving.

title: Hoppers / Appa (plain, 1 piece)
content: Calories: 130-150 kcal. Protein: 2g. Carbs: 20-25g. Fat: 5-7g. Made from rice flour and coconut milk. An egg hopper adds approximately 72 kcal. Typically 2-3 hoppers per meal.

title: Protein Bar (average)
content: Calories: 200-280 kcal. Protein: 15-25g. Carbs: 20-35g. Fat: 7-15g. Convenience protein source but often high in sugar alcohols and added sugars. Check labels carefully. Not a substitute for whole food protein.

title: Steak (Beef Sirloin, cooked, 100g)
content: Calories: 206 kcal. Protein: 26g. Carbs: 0g. Fat: 10.6g. Rich in iron (2.9mg heme iron, highly bioavailable), zinc, B12, and creatine. Red meat should be limited to 1-2 servings per week per WHO guidelines.

title: Pasta (cooked, 1 cup / 140g)
content: Calories: 220 kcal. Protein: 8g. Carbs: 43g. Fat: 1.3g. Moderate glycemic index (49). Whole wheat pasta has more fiber (6.3g vs 2.5g). Good pre-workout carb source for sustained energy.
